name: East Timorese Portuguese

ancestors.notes: European Portuguese colonial base. East Timor (then Portuguese Timor) was a Portuguese colony from 1702 until Indonesian invasion in 1975; Portuguese was restored as official language after independence in 2002.

Tetum (Tetun) adstrate: the true national lingua franca, co-official with Portuguese. Tetum is an Austronesian language with a strong Portuguese loanword vocabulary (Tetum Praça). Heavy phonological transfer into East Timorese Portuguese.

Indonesian adstrate: 24 years of Indonesian occupation (1975–1999) left strong Indonesian influence on vocabulary and some phonological patterns, especially among speakers educated under the occupation.

notes: East Timorese Portuguese (Português Timorense). Co-official language with Tetum (Tetun); spoken as L1 by ~5% and as L2 by ~35% of the population. Portuguese was suppressed during the Indonesian occupation (1975–1999) and reintroduced after independence (2002), creating a generation gap: older speakers learned Portuguese pre-occupation, younger speakers are being educated in it anew. KEY FEATURES: (1) FIVE-VOWEL SYSTEM: /ɨ/ absent; full unstressed vowels. (2) RHOTIC: alveolar [r]; uvular [ʁ] absent or rare. (3) DIGRAPHS: ⟨lh⟩ and ⟨nh⟩ sometimes simplified to [l] and [n] by Tetum speakers. (4) TETUM INFLUENCE: open syllable preference (Tetum is a largely open-syllable language); coda consonants may be weakened. (5) NASAL VOWELS: partially preserved but with Tetum-influenced realisation. (6) The Dili variety (capital) is most prestigious. Sources: Hull (2001), Thomaz (2002), Taylor-Leech (2009).

sources.author: Thomaz, L.F.
sources.title: Babel Loro Sa'e: O problema linguístico de Timor-Leste

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).

allophone_rules.notes: East Timorese Portuguese keeps an alveolar coda /s/ (no Lisbon 'chiado'); this overrides the inherited pt-PT PT_CODA_S_HUSH rule by id, matching the alveolar coda already declared in this spec's positional_graphemes.
East Timorese Portuguese keeps an alveolar coda /z/; overrides the inherited pt-PT PT_CODA_Z_HUSH rule by id.
